steps.title: Complete the square to find the minimum value
steps.content:
For $f(x) = x^2 - 12x + 30$, complete the square by adding and subtracting the square of half the $x$ coefficient.

Half of $-12$ is $-6$, and $(-6)^2 = 36$. So:
$$f(x) = (x^2 - 12x + 36) - 36 + 30 = (x-6)^2 - 6$$

In vertex form $(x-h)^2 + k$, the minimum value is the constant term $k = -6$ (since the leading coefficient \$1 > 0$, the parabola opens upward). That matches choice B — B is correct.
